                                                                   Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100376

                                                           Egyptian Informatics Journal
                                                         journal homepage: www.sciencedirect.com


Full length article

Diagonalize three-dimensional nonlinear chaotic map to encrypt color
image
Mahmoud I. Moussa a,⇑, Eman I. Abd El-Latif b, Ahmed H. Abu El-Atta a
a
    Computer Science Department, Faculty of Computer and Artificial Intelligence, Benha University, Egypt
b
    Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Faculty of Science, Benha University, Egypt




a r t i c l e           i n f o                            a b s t r a c t

Article history:                                          In this paper, multidimensional chaos systems called a trilinear chaotic system for encryption the color
Received 17 October 2021                                  image is used. Combining the multi-dimensional chaotic system with encryption algorithms improves
Revised 26 March 2022                                     the security. The trilinear system is built from logistic and cubic maps and generates six completely ran-
Accepted 1 May 2023
                                                          dom bijections T 1 ðxÞ; T 2 ðxÞ; T 2 ðyÞ; T 3 ðxÞ; T 3 ðyÞ; and T 3 ðzÞ: The map T 1 ðxÞ randomly shuffles the image pix-
Available online 7 June 2023
                                                          els, the two maps T 2 ðxÞ and T 2 ðyÞ scramble the position of the pixels, and the other three maps change the
                                                          pixel values. The correlations among the three components of RGB components are reduced, and the
Keywords:
                                                          security of the algorithm increases. The simulation results demonstrated that the procedure has very
Chaotic system
Cryptography
                                                          large key spaces and a high level of security.
Image encryption                                           Ó 2023 THE AUTHORS. Published by Elsevier BV on behalf of Faculty of Computers and Artificial Intel-
Trilinear system                                             ligence, Cairo University. This is an open access article under the CC BY-NC-ND license (http://creative-
                                                                                                                                             commons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/).




1. Introduction                                                                              T 1 ðxÞ; T 2 ðxÞ; and T 2 ðyÞ to create three M  N matrix keys (M r , Mb ;
                                                                                             Mg ), and it inputs the other three maps T 3 ðxÞ; ; T 3 ðyÞ; and T 3 ðzÞ to
    A variety picture consists of many lines and segments of pixels;                         output three vector keys (V r ; V b ; V g ) of length MN. Utilizing vector
these pixels have specific values that address the level of the vari-                        keys, the rows and columns of images are shuffled and scrambled.
ety in the picture. As a result, the color image is represented as a                         While the remainder keys are applied many times to growth the
three numerical matrix for each small area to define the color.                              difficulty and the security of the system. The first matrix M r is per-
Image security is a crucial issue because sensitive images can invite                        formed to shuffle the block of the image and the remainder matrix
attacks from different places. The transformation of a plain image                           Mb ; M g scramble the location in rows and columns, respectively.
into a different cipher image is the goal of image encryption. Due                           Finally, in order to increase the system’s complexity and security,
to its sensitivity to initial parameters, high security and totally ran-                     the three vector keys (V r ; V b ; V g ) are utilized to twice alter the val-
dom performance, chaos theory is used in image encryption. Mat-                              ues of the matrix.
thews created the first chaotic system in 1989 [1]. Numerous
scientific algorithms have defined image encryption with a few                               2. List of contributions
secret parameters based on one- or two-dimensional chaotic sys-
tems. This work conquers the past works by offering a totally irreg-                            In this paper, the authors developed new techniques based on
ular multi-layered framework with different mystery boundaries.                              the trilinear chaotic system to clearly increase the key space size
The presented chaotic system simultaneously encrypts RGB image                               and the robustness of the encryption techniques of digital images
and their components. The proposed chaos system consists of six                              correlated. List of the advantages of this work are given below:
maps T 1 ðxÞ; T 2 ðxÞ; T 2 ðyÞ; T 3 ðxÞ; ; T 3 ðyÞ; and T 3 ðzÞ to increase conju-
                                2
gation of the items x3i ; xi , y2i , xi yi , y3i ; z2i xi ;    z3i and expand the secu-      The proposed chaotic system uses the quadratic and cubic cou-
rity of the system. Initially, the proposed Pixel Transform                                      pling of the items xi ; yi andzi .
Table (PTT) procedure inputs the first three maps                                               The diagonalizations of the proposed system (2) increase the

  The proposed cryptosystem runs randomly multi-level diffusion            shuffling. Color images have received a lot of attention because
   of matrix values.                                                        they are full of information. The density of RGB components in
  The proposed approach increases the secret key space up to               the color image is determined by the numerical values of RGB com-
   10415 :, which is the highest.                                           ponents found in each pixel [14]. There have been numerous
  Anaconda 4.8.3 is used to implement the algorithm using                  descriptions of encryption algorithms [6,11,15,16,18,23,27].
   Python programming language.                                             Because they disregard the correlations that exist among color
  The computational study between the proposed algorithm with              components, these algorithms are more susceptible to attack.
   some pervious works shows high competitiveness regarding                 Utilizing 2D Hénon-Sine map to generate a pixel permutation,
   statistical analysis and security.                                       Wu et al. [24] suggested an algorithm based on pixel diffusion
                                                                            and a DNA approach in 2018. Wu et al. [25] encrypted an asym-
                                                                            metric multi-image using compressed sensing and a nonlinear
3. Related works                                                            operation in the cylindrical diffraction domain. In 2020, Yasser,
                                                                            et al. [27], produced approach to encrypt images that shuffles pix-
    Over the past few decades, chaos-theoretic encryption algo-             els and performs substitution operations with a chaotic system and
rithms have emerged as a powerful strategy for increasing security.         DWT. El Shafai et al. [28] introduced a DNA encoding method for
By far most picture encryption calculations have been presented in          medical images based on a piecewise linear chaotic system.
view of ID &2D dimensional chaotic maps. Zhang et al. in 2005 [3]
presented an algorithm for encrypting the given image that was              4. Proposed chaos system
depend on a chaotic map and a large encryption key by using a
pixel shambling process applied an induced chaotic permutation                  Fig. 1 shows the geometric visualisation of the trilinear interpo-
matrix. Chong et al. [4] use Lorenz of a 3D chaotic system to

improve image cryptography’s performance and security. Xiang-

lation utilizing an arranging change of a turbulent grouping to get

address codes for picture interpretation. The disadvantages of

                                                                                                Finding a new x0 ; y0 ; z0 system requires diagonalizing the equa-
                                                                                             tions in (2). In system (2), the diagonalizations of the quadratic
                                                                                                                               T          T           T
                                                                                             form in 1D and 2D are x0nþ1 Hx0nþ1 x0nþ1 Dx0nþ1 , y0nþ1 Qy0nþ1 ,
                                                                                             where H; D and Q are 2  2 matrices within four parameters for
                                                                                             each. In addition, the number of parameters increases to nine when
                                                                                             the three variables x; y; and z are diagonalized in their 3D form
                                                                                             [11].


                                                                                             6. The proposed algorithm

                                                                                                A. Key Generation
Fig. 3. The performance of the 3D chaotic map in first 1000 iteration at k = 3.54, b ¼          The RGB image is converted to three parts with M  N matrices,
0.036, and a ¼ 0:039 in xyz space.                                                           with M rows and N columns of pixels for every component. Three
                                                                                         3
M.I. Moussa, E.I. Abd El-Latif and A.H. Abu El-Atta                                                                  Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100376


M  N matrix keys (M r , M b ; M g ), and three vector keys (V x ; V y ; V z )       Bb ¼ M b B ðMb ÞT mod 256
of length MN are generated by the proposed system used a new
method called Pixel Transform Table (PTT). In addition, three
                                                                                               T
                                                                                     Gg ¼ Mg G M g mod 256
sequences of real numbers are generated by T 1 ðxÞ; T 2 ðxÞ; andT 2 ðyÞ
and then converted to (M r , M b , M g ), and the chaotic maps T 3 ðxÞ;                 In every matrix (Rr ; Bb ; Gg Þ, odd index ð2k þ 1Þ row is exchanged
T 3 ðyÞ; and T 3 ðzÞ produce three extra sequences which are changed                 with an even index ð2kÞ row, and repeat this process for the col-
into (V x ; V y ; V z ). The PTT algorithm is displayed below.                       umns, as shown in Fig. 6. We replication the exchange process r-
PTT Algorithm                                                                        times and become (Rr ðrÞ; Bb ðrÞ; Gg ðrÞÞ.
                                                                                        Phase 2: Utilizing the two vector keys ðV x ; V y Þ; the positions of
                                                                                     pixel were muddled in columns and rows during this phase. We
  Input: The chaotic equation                                                        apply the second diffusion level by dividing the values of the com-
  Output: T j map                                                                    ponents Rr ðrÞ; Bb ðrÞ; and Gg ðrÞ by their size MN to produce the val-
  1. Set the system’s chaotic parameters.                                            ues of the pixels, i.e.
  2. Repeat the Eq. (2) to create Sj ðiÞ sequences

      (V x ; V y ; V z ) of length MN, respectively.                                 matrices; RyðrÞ; ByðrÞ and GyðrÞ:

                                                                                        P now is the current plain pixel value, Dpre is the previous cipher
                                                                                     pixel value following the current diffusion, and Ppre is the previous
                                                                                     plain value. Dnow is the current ciphered pixel value following the
    B. Image Encryption Algorithm (IEA)
                                                                                     current diffusion. Their underlying qualities in RzðrÞ; BzðrÞ; and
                                                                                     GzðrÞ are equivalent to nothing. Monitor columns in P3 to set three
    IEA algorithm involves three phases as follows:
                                                                                     vectors VðRzðrÞÞ, VðBzðrÞÞ, and VðGzðrÞÞ each with length MN.
IEA : PlainImageðPÞ ! CipherImageP5                                                  Utilizing the vector product with the key vector V z , we apply the
                                                                                     third level of pixel diffusion to these vectors in the following
   Phase 1:
                                                                                     manner:
   Along with the alteration of pixels’ values, we describe a secure
block shuffling tool. The first diffusion level, or random change in                 8 r                   
RBG pixel values, is the crucial effect of PPT in this process: The                  < Zz ¼ VðRzðrÞÞ  V g mod256

Random Diffusion algorithm

verted image P5 of size M  N.                                                       Br ðrÞ ¼ bq:R0 ðrÞc mod 256

    C. Image Decryption Algorithm (IDA)                                              Bb ðrÞ ¼ bq:B0 ðrÞc mod 256                                                  ð14Þ

                                                                                     Gg ðrÞ ¼ bq:G0 ðrÞc mod 256
                                                                                       Step 6: To get rid of the effect of randomly switching the col-
                                                                                     umns and rows, return to the reverse paths.
                                                                                       Step 7: The following is an inverted first random diffusion:

   The plain image P is recovered through these seven steps.                                 For a small constant, the running time of the PPT is
Finally, IDA can be denoted by the following formula:                                      Oðq ¼ MN þ Þ, and the running time of steps 1 through 7 is
   IDA: Cipher Image P5 ! Plain Image (P).                                                 OðMNÞ which is a linear function of the image’s size. The experi-

M.I. Moussa, E.I. Abd El-Latif and A.H. Abu El-Atta                                                                        Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100376


                                                                                       0.036, a ¼ 0.039, x0 ¼ 3.4 1018 , y0 ¼ 4.5 1018 , z0 ¼ 5.6 1018 ),
                                                                                       to encrypt the input image of the 256  256 ‘‘Baboon” and ‘‘Lena”
                                                                                       images, as depicted in Fig. 9(a – d). Fig. 10(a – c) depicts the ‘‘ba-
                                                                                       boon,” ‘‘Lena,” and their components prior to encryption. The his-
                                                                                       tograms show the correlation among the pixels in the plain
                                                                                       images ‘‘baboon” and ‘‘Lena” at each level of color density.
                                                                                          The encrypted two images are depicted in Fig. 11 (a–d) and the
                                                                                       histograms of two images are depicted in Fig. 12(a – c). The his-
                                                                                       tograms show how the ciphered RGB pixels are consistently corre-
                                                                                       lated with the pixels at each level.


                                                                                       8. Security analysis

         Fig. 11. The ciphered of Baboon‘‘ and ”Lena‘‘ images from (a-d).                 A. The Key Size
                                                                                          Brute-force attacks must be rendered ineffective by the encryp-
                                                                                       tion’s whole number of special keys. Six values are used in our
mental procedure determines the practicability of our IEA/IDA
approach’s consumption time.
   The processor Intel(R) Center (TM) i7-8550U computer chip @
1.80 GHz 2.00 GHz and 8 GB Slam is used in IEA/IDA is practicable.
On a 256 image P/P 5 of size 256  256, the encryption and decryp-
tion processes are carried out with the help of an 8 GB RAM and an
Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8550U CPU running at 1.80 GHz 2.00 GHz. The
average time to consume is<4.01 ms. Our running opportunity is
near the running time in [2].
                                                                                                  Fig. 13. Result of decrypted image using correct parameter.



7. Experimental results

   Anaconda 4.8.3 is used to implement the system. We have cho-
sen Python as the programming language for our development. An
Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8550U CPU running at 1.80 GHz 2.00 GHz and
8 GB of RAM are used to test the implementation in Windows 10
64-bit OS. Take the underlying boundaries and values: 1D
(l ¼ 6:27; x0 ¼ 1.2 1018 ), 2D (l1 ¼ 6:27, l2 ¼ 3.18, c1 ¼
0.111,c2 ¼ 0.111,x0 ¼ 2:3  1018 ; y0 ¼ 1.2 1018 ), 3D (k = 3.54, b=                             Fig. 14. Result of decrypted image using wrong parameter.




                                                       Fig. 12. (a–c): Histograms after encryption for two images.

M.I. Moussa, E.I. Abd El-Latif and A.H. Abu El-Atta                                                                                                  Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100376


system:x1D 0 ; x0 ; y0 ; x0 ; y0 , z0
                  2D   2D   3D  3D   3D
                                        and the eight parameters of l,                               Table 2
l1 ; l2 ; c1 ; c2 ; k; b; a; as secret keys. Wang et al. [25] proved that                            the correlation values for images (H/D).

if the precision is 1017 , the keys K x1D = K x2D ¼ K y2D ¼ K x3D ¼ K y3D ¼                           Image             H/D           Plain image            Cipher Image

                                                                                                       Lena              R             0.9783                 0.0019                0.00092
   Let the input images have size 256256. The number of itera-                                                          G             0.9795                  0.0036                0.0038
tions over six maps I0 is 6  ð3  M  N) = 6  ð3  256  256 )                                                        B             0.9594                 0.00068               0.0020
                                                                                       235
220  107 : The key space spreads to  1:953  107  10                                      ¼         Baboon            R             0.9413                 0.0018                 0.0062
                  242                                          138     58        140                                     G             0.8796                 0.0056                0.0060
1:953  10              . the space of key is larger than 2          , 2 , 10          ; 2256
                                                                                                                         B             0.9164                  0.0019                 0.0077
[17,26,30]. It is greater than 2448 = 7.810134 , the highest number
of key spaces cited in the study [23]. Within the diagonalization
form (2), there are 24 initial values. Our key space increments it
up to 10415 . The key space described by the proposed algorithms
is large enough to withstand brute-force attacks.                                                    Table 3
                                                                                                     the correlation values for images (D/D).

       B. The Sensitivity Examination of the Secret Keys.                                              Image             D/D        Plain image             Cipher Image
                                                                                                                                                            Proposed approach     Ref [10]
   Little differences between keys generate another cipher images.
                                                                                                       Lena              R          0.9685                  0.0013               0.0008482
The Baboon’s decrypted image with the correct key of k = 3.66 is                                                         G          0.9574                   0.0019               0.0008482
depicted in Fig. 13. On the other hand, the Baboon image is                                                              B          0.8994                  0.00031              0.0008482
decrypted in Fig. 14 using an incorrect encryption key equal to                                        Baboon            R          0.6471                  0.0022                0.00370914
k = 3.66000000000000001. The algorithm was made more sensi-                                                              G          0.9567                  0.0035                0.00370914
tive to the key thanks to this. A little change in the key will bring                                                    B          0.9355                   0.0013                0.00370914
about a completely unique unscrambling result, and the attacker
will not have the option to get to the right plain picture.

       C. Adjacent Pixels Correlation Analysis
                                                                                                       Horizontal direction (H/D),
    The following formula is used to calculate the vertical, horizon-                                  Vertical direction (V/D),
tal, and diagonal correlation between 3000 randomly selected                                           Diagonal direction (D/D).
nearby pixels:
                                                                                                        They show a lot of concentration in the plain image and have a
          1X N
EðxÞ ¼          ðxi Þ                                                                   ð16Þ         tendency of one in two neighboring pixels in the ciphered image.
          N i¼1                                                                                      This indicates that the neighboring pixels in the ciphered image
                                                                                                     are random, and the encryption effects resist statistical attack.
          1 XN
EðyÞ ¼         ðy Þ                                                                     ð17Þ             D. Detection of different Attack
          N i¼1 i

                                                                                                        To calculate the capacity, the Pixel Change Rate Number, and
           X
           N
DðxÞ ¼            ðxi  EðxÞÞ2                                                          ð18Þ         the Unified Average Change Intensity (UACI) tests are applied.
           i¼1                                                                                       The UACI test compares the average intensity of two images, while
                                                                                                     the NPCR test distinguishes between the numbers of distinct pixels
           X
           N                                                                                         in each [13]. UACI and NPCR can calculated as following:
DðyÞ ¼            ðyi  EðyÞÞ2                                                          ð19Þ

            i¼1                                                                                                        j

                                                                                                     UACIR; G; B ¼ @          255         A  100                                            ð22Þ

                  N i¼1

                                                                                                                             ij DR;G;B ði; jÞ

        DðxÞ DðyÞ
                                                                                                                         (

   Lena                 R        0.9238         0.0023                         0.0016              UACIR; G; B greater than 33.44%. The previous work demonstrate that
                        G        0.9479          0.0027                         0.0011              our method is extremely sensitive to minute changes in the input

                                                                                                         E. PSNR Analysis

M.I. Moussa, E.I. Abd El-Latif and A.H. Abu El-Atta                                                                          Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100376

                                                                                    NPCR and UACI Results.

                                                                                      Image              Proposed approach                         Ref [10]
                                                                                                                 NPCR%            UACI %           NPCR%         UACI %
                                                                                      Lena               R       99.5157          33.4228          99.6052       33.4132
                                                                                                         G       99.4792          33.4441

                                                                                    Table 5
                                                                                    PSNR Results.

                                                                                      Image          Proposed approach                       Reff [20]
                                                                                                     R            G           B              R           G         B
                                                                                      Lena           7.968        8.888       9.759          7.8992      8.576     9.678
                                                                                      Baboon         8.398        9.457       8.970          8.958       9.414     8.415


                                                                                                                        255
                                                                                    PSNRR; G; B ¼ 20 log 10 ð                        Þ                                 ð25Þ
                                                                                                                   sqrtðMSER; G; B Þ

                                                                                                    X X C R; G; B ði; jÞ  C 0R;G;B ði; jÞ
                                                                                    MSER; G; B ¼                                                                       ð26Þ
                                                                                                     i       j
                                                                                                                           MxN

                                                                                       The mean square error (MSE) portrays the distinction in the
                                                                                    qualities from [0 255] among the plain and the encoded picture.
                                                                                    Table 5 also shows the differences in PSNR values among the orig-
                                                                                    inal and encrypted image. Statistical attacks are more resistant
                                                                                    with our method.


                                                                                    9. Conclusion

                                                                                       For image security, we recommended a key space algorithm
                                                                                    that could withstand brute-force attacks. Three keys are generated
                                                                                    by the proposed cryptosystem in the form of a MN-length vector
                                                                                    matrix and three keys in the M  N square matrix. Multi-level dif-
                                                                                    fusion of pixel values and variety among shuffling and scrambling
                                                                                    of rows and columns in the color components of the plain image
                                                                                    are the foundations of our cryptosystem for image. The computa-
                                                                                    tional comparison of the proposed procedure to other cryptosys-
                                                                                    tems revealed that the proposed system has large key spaces and
                                                                                    a high level of security. The benefit of the current system is utiliz-
                                                                                    ing staggered encryption in view of huge key space.

                                                                                    Declaration of Competing Interest

                                                                                        The authors declare that they have no known competing finan-
                                                                                    cial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared
                                                                                    to influence the work reported in this paper.
